sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application 319 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.1 2. For hearing of CMA No.1937/2025 3. For hearing of CMA No.1939/2025 4. For hearing of main case 29.10.2025 Mr. Shams Mohiuddin Ansari, advocate for the applicant Syed Ahsan Ali Shah, advocate for respondent On 22.10.2025, following order was passed: Per learned counsel, matter is squarely covered by judgment of the Supreme Court in the case of Commissioner Inland Revenue Lahore vs. Millat Tractors Limited, Lahore and others reported as 2024 SCMR 700. Despite a fixed date was given and respondent remains unrepresented despite appearance having been entered on his behalf on the last date. As an indulgence, adjourned to 29.10.2025. Interim orders passed earlier to continue till the next date. Today respective learned counsel jointly submit that it may be just and proper for the impugned order to be set aside and the matter be remanded back to the learned Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
